titleStmt.title: Transcription and Translation of NRS GD55/218

availability.p: Status unknown. Exported for research and migration workflows.

revisionDesc.change: transcription moa=487 status=live modified=2021-04-07 07:53:57.707124+00 | translation moa=488 status=live modified=2021-04-07 07:53:56.313607+00

p.cl: Omnibus Sancte Matris Ecclesie filiis presentibus et futuris . Philippus De pethcox Salutem .

Sciatis me dedisse et Concessisse . et hac mea
Carta confirmasse ex bona uoluntate . et assensu . philippi filii mei et heredis Deo et ecclesie Sancte Marie De Melros et Monachis
ibidem deo seruientibus [written ˊseruientibusˊ deo˝ with transposition marks] . pro animabus Dominorum meorum . Cospatricii . et Waldeui . Comitum De Dunbar . et pro animabus patris
et Matris mee . et Omnium antecessorum meorum . et pro salute Domini mei . P . Comitis de Dunbar . et pro salute mea . et P . filii mei et heredis .
et Omnium successorum meorum . terram illam in territorio De Pethcox que iacet inter diuisas terre Monachorum de herteshevede
et Riuulum qui nominatur Prestmunethburne . quam predicti Monachi ex donatione mea . et precepto per predictum Riuulum
terre sue de hereshevede incluserunt . et Decem acras in culturam De Beleside terre sue de Edmundist’ propinuius adiacentes
in liberam . puram . et perpetuam elemosinam . Ita libere et quiete . plene et honorifice . sicut aliquam elemosinam liberius . quietius .
plenius et honorificentius tenent . et possident . Et ego et heredes . mei manutenebimus . et warantizabimus predictam terram
prefatis Monachis contra omnes homines . in perpetuum . et adquietabimus erga dominum Regem et omnes alios ab omni seruicio
et consuetudine . et exactione seculari .

Testibus . Comiti . Patricio Domino meo . Patricio filio eius . Bernardus fraser . Rogero De Merlay . Adam
de powlew’ . Adam de Gord’ . Thoma de Neseb’ . Philippo filio meo . et herede . Waldeuo persona de withinham . Magistro Willelmo
de Ercheld’ . Magistro Iohanne persona . Et Multis Aliis .
To all sons of Holy Mother Church, present and future, Philip of Pitcox sends greeting.

Know me to have given and granted, and by this my charter to have made firm, out of the good will and agreement of Philip my son and heir, to God and to the church of St Mary at Melrose, and to the monks in that place serving God, for the souls of my lords, Cospatric, and Waldef, earls of Dunbar, and for the souls of my father and mother, and of all my ancestors, and for the salvation of my lord, Patrick, earl of Dunbar, and for my own salvation, and of Philip my son and heir, and of all my successors, the land in the territory of Pitcox which lies between the boundaries of the monks' land at Hartside and the little stream that is called Pressmennan Burn, which the aforesaid monks have annexed along the aforesaid stream to their own land at Hartside by reason of my donation and instruction; and ten acres in ploughland at 'Beleside' most closely adjacent to their land at Edmundstoun; in free, pure, and perpetual alms; as freely and quitely, fully and honourably, as they freely, quitely, fully and honourably hold and possess any alms. And I and my heirs shall maintain and warrant the said land to the aforementioned monks against all men for ever, and we shall discharge claims in respect of the lord king and all others from every service and custom and secular exaction.

As witnesses: Earl Patrick my lord; Patrick his son; Bernard Fraser; Roger de Merlay; Adam of Polwarth; Adam of Gordon; Thomas of Nisbet; Philip my son and heir; Waldef parson of Whittinghame; Master William of Earlston; Master John, parson; and many others.
